Video Codec Bitmask Constants

Bitmasks for video codec capabilities

Each codec is represented by a single bit, allowing multiple codecs to be advertised simultaneously in the codec_capabilities_video field.
#define VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_ALL (VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_RGBA | VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_H265 | VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_JPEG)
Default video codec capabilities (all codecs supported).

Audio Codec Bitmask Constants

Bitmasks for audio codec capabilities

Each codec is represented by a single bit, allowing multiple codecs to be advertised simultaneously in the codec_capabilities_audio field.
#define AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_ALL (AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_RAW | AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_OPUS)
Default audio codec capabilities (all codecs supported). #define VIDEO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(cap, codec)
Check if a video codec is supported in capabilities bitmask. #define AUDIO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(cap, codec)
Check if an audio codec is supported in capabilities bitmask.

Detailed Description

This header provides FFmpeg integration for decoding various media formats. It wraps FFmpeg's complex API into a simple interface for extracting RGB video frames and float audio samples.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Multi-format container support (mp4, avi, mkv, webm, etc.)
  • Video decoding to RGB24 format
  • Audio decoding to 48kHz mono float
  • Stdin input support via custom AVIOContext
  • Seeking for loop support
  • Automatic stream detection

FFMPEG LIBRARIES USED:

  • libavformat: Container demuxing
  • libavcodec: Video/audio codec decoding
  • libavutil: Utilities and error handling
  • libswscale: Video format conversion (YUV -> RGB)
  • libswresample: Audio resampling (any rate -> 48kHz mono)

SUPPORTED CODECS:

  • Video: H.264, H.265, VP8, VP9, AV1, MPEG-4, etc.
  • Audio: AAC, MP3, Opus, Vorbis, FLAC, PCM, etc.
  • Containers: MP4, AVI, MKV, WebM, MOV, FLV, GIF, etc.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides a unified interface for media sources in ascii-chat, abstracting over webcams, media files, and stdin input. This allows the capture thread to work with any media source transparently.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Unified API for webcam, files, and stdin
  • FFmpeg-based decoding for all common formats
  • Video frame extraction (RGB format)
  • Audio sample extraction (48kHz float mono)
  • Loop support for file playback
  • Automatic format detection

SUPPORTED SOURCES:

  • MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM: Hardware webcam device
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE: Media file (mp4, avi, mkv, gif, mp3, etc.)
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN: Piped or redirected media data
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST: Test pattern (existing)

SUPPORTED FORMATS:

  • Video: mp4, avi, mkv, webm, mov, flv, wmv, gif (animated)
  • Audio: mp3, aac, opus, flac, wav, ogg, m4a
  • Images: gif, png, jpg (static - single frame)

USAGE: 

// Create media source from file
media_source_t *source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, "video.mp4");
media_source_set_loop(source, true);
// Read video frames
while (!done) {


    image_t *frame = media_source_read_video(source);


    if (!frame) {


        if (media_source_at_end(source)) break;


        continue; // Error, try next frame


    }


    // Process frame...
}
// Read audio samples
float buffer[480];
size_t samples = media_source_read_audio(source, buffer, 480);
// Cleanup
media_source_destroy(source);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Typedef
  Documentation




typedef
  struct ffmpeg_decoder_t ffmpeg_decoder_t


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Opaque FFmpeg decoder handle. Forward declaration of FFmpeg
    decoder structure. Use ffmpeg_decoder_create() to create and
    ffmpeg_decoder_destroy() to cleanup.


Definition at line 58 of file ffmpeg_decoder.h.






typedef
  struct media_source_t media_source_t


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Opaque media source handle. Forward declaration of media source
    structure. Use media_source_create() to create and
    media_source_destroy() to cleanup.










THREAD
  SAFETY: 


    
  
  • media_source_read_video() and media_source_read_audio() may
      be called from different threads (e.g., video render thread + audio
      callback thread)
    • 
  
  • Both operations access the shared FFmpeg decoder
    • 
  
  • FFmpeg decoders are NOT thread-safe for concurrent operations
    • 
  
  • media_source_t uses internal synchronization to protect decoder
      access
    • 
  
  • Callers do NOT need external locking
    • 



Definition at line 105 of file source.h.

ffmpeg_decoder_t * ffmpeg_decoder_create (const char *
  path)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Create FFmpeg decoder from file path.


Parameters


path File path to media file (must not be
  NULL)


Returns


Pointer to decoder, or NULL on failure


Creates and initializes an FFmpeg decoder for the specified file.
    Opens the file, detects streams, and initializes codecs.




Initialization process:



  
1.

  
Open format context (avformat_open_input)

  
2.

  
Find stream information (avformat_find_stream_info)

  
3.

  
Detect video and audio streams

  
4.

  
Open codec contexts for detected streams

  
5.

  
Initialize swscale/swresample contexts




Note


Sets errno context on failure



Call ffmpeg_decoder_destroy() to cleanup




Definition at line 590 of file
    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


590                                                           {
591   if (!path) {
592     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Path is NULL");
593     return NULL;
594   }
595 
596   // Suppress FFmpeg's verbose debug logging (H.264 codec warnings, etc.)
597   // Only set this once, it's a global setting
598   static bool ffmpeg_log_level_set = false;
599   if (!ffmpeg_log_level_set) {
600     av_log_set_level(AV_LOG_QUIET);                  // Suppress all FFmpeg logging
601     av_log_set_callback(ffmpeg_silent_log_callback); // Install silent callback to discard all output
602     ffmpeg_log_level_set = true;
603   }
604 
605   ffmpeg_decoder_t *decoder = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(ffmpeg_decoder_t), ffmpeg_decoder_t *);
606   if (!decoder) {
607     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate decoder");
608     return NULL;
609   }
610 
611   memset(decoder, 0, sizeof(*decoder));
612   decoder->video_stream_idx = -1;
613   decoder->audio_stream_idx = -1;
614   decoder->last_video_pts = -1.0;
615   decoder->last_audio_pts = -1.0;
616 
617   // Capture FFmpeg's probing output (FFmpeg may write directly to either stream)
618   int ret = 0;
619   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
620     // Configure FFmpeg options for HTTP streaming performance
621     AVDictionary *options = NULL;
622 
623     // For HTTP/HTTPS streams: enable fast probing and reconnection (validated via production-grade URL regex)
624     if (path && url_is_valid(path)) {
625       // Limit probing to 32KB for faster format detection
626       av_dict_set(&options, "probesize", "32768", 0);
627       // Analyze for 100ms max to determine streams quickly
628       av_dict_set(&options, "analyzeduration", "100000", 0);
629       // Enable auto-reconnection for interrupted connections
630       av_dict_set(&options, "reconnect", "1", 0);
631       // Allow reconnection for streamed protocols
632       av_dict_set(&options, "reconnect_streamed", "1", 0);
633       // Set reasonable I/O timeout (10 seconds)
634       av_dict_set(&options, "rw_timeout", "10000000", 0);
635       // Enable HTTP persistent connection (keep-alive) for better performance
636       av_dict_set(&options, "http_persistent", "1", 0);
637       // Reduce connect timeout to fail faster if server is unreachable
638       av_dict_set(&options, "connect_timeout", "5000000", 0);
639     }
640 
641     // Open input file
642     ret = avformat_open_input(&decoder->format_ctx, path, NULL, &options);
643     av_dict_free(&options); // Free options dictionary
644 
645     if (ret >= 0) {
646       // Find stream info
647       if (avformat_find_stream_info(decoder->format_ctx, NULL) < 0) {
648         ret = -1;
649       }
650     }
651   });
652 
653   if (ret < 0) {
654     if (ret == -1) {
655       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to find stream info");
656     } else {
657       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_OPEN, "Failed to open media file: %s", path);
658     }
659     if (decoder->format_ctx) {
660       avformat_close_input(&decoder->format_ctx);
661     }
662     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
663     return NULL;
664   }
665 
666   // Install interrupt callback to allow seeking to interrupt long av_read_frame() calls
667   // Do this AFTER finding stream info to ensure format context is fully initialized
668   if (decoder->format_ctx) {
669     decoder->format_ctx->interrupt_callback.callback = ffmpeg_interrupt_callback;
670     decoder->format_ctx->interrupt_callback.opaque = decoder;
671   }
672 
673   // Open video codec
674   asciichat_error_t err = open_codec_context(decoder->format_ctx, AVMEDIA_TYPE_VIDEO, &decoder->video_stream_idx,
675                                              &decoder->video_codec_ctx);
676   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
677     log_warn("Failed to open video codec (file may be audio-only)");
678   }
679 
680   // Open audio codec - audio is enabled by default (no option needed)
681   // Always try to open audio codec, don't rely on GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) which has a default issue
682   err = open_codec_context(decoder->format_ctx, AVMEDIA_TYPE_AUDIO, &decoder->audio_stream_idx,
683                            &decoder->audio_codec_ctx);
684   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
685     log_debug("No audio codec found (file may be video-only or audio codec not available)");
686     decoder->audio_stream_idx = -1;
687     decoder->audio_codec_ctx = NULL;
688   }
689 
690   // Require at least one stream
691   if (decoder->video_stream_idx < 0 && decoder->audio_stream_idx < 0) {
692     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "No video or audio streams found");
693     ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
694     return NULL;
695   }
696 
697   // Allocate frame and packet
698   decoder->frame = av_frame_alloc();
699   decoder->packet = av_packet_alloc();
700   if (!decoder->frame || !decoder->packet) {
701     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
702     ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
703     return NULL;
704   }
705 
706   // Initialize swscale context for video if present
707   if (decoder->video_codec_ctx) {
708     // Validate codec context has valid dimensions and pixel format
709     // For HTTP streams, these might not be valid until first frame is read
710     if (decoder->video_codec_ctx->width <= 0 || decoder->video_codec_ctx->height <= 0) {
711       log_warn("Video codec has invalid dimensions (%dx%d), will initialize swscale on first frame",
712                decoder->video_codec_ctx->width, decoder->video_codec_ctx->height);
713       // Don't create swscale context yet - will create it lazily on first frame read
714     } else if (decoder->video_codec_ctx->pix_fmt == AV_PIX_FMT_NONE) {
715       log_warn("Video codec has invalid pixel format, will initialize swscale on first frame");
716       // Don't create swscale context yet - will create it lazily on first frame read
717     } else {
718       // Create swscale context with valid parameters
719       LOG_IO("swscaler", {
720         decoder->sws_ctx =
721             sws_getContext(decoder->video_codec_ctx->width, decoder->video_codec_ctx->height,
722                            decoder->video_codec_ctx->pix_fmt, decoder->video_codec_ctx->width,
723                            decoder->video_codec_ctx->height, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, SWS_BILINEAR, NULL, NULL, NULL);
724       });
725       if (!decoder->sws_ctx) {
726         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to create swscale context");
727         ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
728         return NULL;
729       }
730     }
731   }
732 
733   // Initialize swresample context for audio if present
734   if (decoder->audio_codec_ctx) {
735     // Store output sample rate for position tracking
736     decoder->audio_sample_rate = TARGET_SAMPLE_RATE;
737 
738     // Allocate resampler context
739     decoder->swr_ctx = swr_alloc();
740     if (!decoder->swr_ctx) {
741       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate swresample context");
742       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
743       return NULL;
744     }
745 
746     // Set options
747     av_opt_set_chlayout(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_chlayout", &decoder->audio_codec_ctx->ch_layout, 0);
748     av_opt_set_int(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_sample_rate", decoder->audio_codec_ctx->sample_rate, 0);
749     av_opt_set_sample_fmt(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_sample_fmt", decoder->audio_codec_ctx->sample_fmt, 0);
750 
751     AVChannelLayout out_ch_layout = AV_CHANNEL_LAYOUT_MONO;
752     av_opt_set_chlayout(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_chlayout", &out_ch_layout, 0);
753     av_opt_set_int(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_sample_rate", TARGET_SAMPLE_RATE, 0);
754     av_opt_set_sample_fmt(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_sample_fmt", AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT, 0);
755 
756     // Initialize
757     if (swr_init(decoder->swr_ctx) < 0) {
758       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to initialize swresample context");
759       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
760       return NULL;
761     }
762 
763     // Allocate audio buffer (10 seconds worth)
764     decoder->audio_buffer_size = TARGET_SAMPLE_RATE * 10;
765     decoder->audio_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(decoder->audio_buffer_size * sizeof(float), float *);
766     if (!decoder->audio_buffer) {
767       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate audio buffer");
768       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
769       return NULL;
770     }
771   }
772 
773   // Initialize video frame prefetching system (for YouTube streaming)
774   if (decoder->video_stream_idx >= 0) {
775     int width = decoder->video_codec_ctx->width;
776     int height = decoder->video_codec_ctx->height;
777 
778     // Create two prefetch image buffers for double-buffering
779     decoder->prefetch_image_a = image_new((size_t)width, (size_t)height);
780     decoder->prefetch_image_b = image_new((size_t)width, (size_t)height);
781     if (!decoder->prefetch_image_a || !decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
782       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate prefetch image buffers");
783       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
784       return NULL;
785     }
786 
787     decoder->current_prefetch_image = decoder->prefetch_image_a;
788     decoder->prefetch_frame_ready = false;
789 
790     // Initialize prefetch mutex
791     if (mutex_init(&decoder->prefetch_mutex, "ffmpeg_prefetch") != 0) {
792       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize prefetch mutex");
793       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
794       return NULL;
795     }
796 
797     if (cond_init(&decoder->prefetch_cond, "ffmpeg_prefetch") != 0) {
798       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize prefetch condition variable");
799       mutex_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
800       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
801       return NULL;
802     }
803 
804     if (mutex_init(&decoder->read_frame_mutex, "ffmpeg_read_frame") != 0) {
805       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize read_frame mutex");
806       mutex_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
807       cond_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_cond);
808       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
809       return NULL;
810     }
811 
812     decoder->prefetch_thread_running = false;
813     decoder->prefetch_should_stop = false;
814   }
815 
816   log_debug("FFmpeg decoder opened: %s (video=%s, audio=%s)", path, decoder->video_stream_idx >= 0 ? "yes" : "no",
817             decoder->audio_stream_idx >= 0 ? "yes" : "no");
818 
819   char decoder_name[256];
820   snprintf(decoder_name, sizeof(decoder_name), "%s", path);
821   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_DECODER(decoder, decoder_name, NULL);
822 
823   return decoder;
824 }

Referenced by media_source_create().






ffmpeg_decoder_t * ffmpeg_decoder_create_stdin (void
  )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Create FFmpeg decoder from stdin.


Returns


Pointer to decoder, or NULL on failure


Creates an FFmpeg decoder that reads from stdin using a custom
    AVIOContext. This allows piping media data directly into ascii-chat.




Usage:




cat video.mp4 | ascii-chat client --file -
ffmpeg -i input.avi -f matroska - | ascii-chat client --file -



Limitations:


    
  
  • Cannot seek (no loop support)
    • 
  
  • Some formats may not work well with stdin (require seekable input)
    • 
  
  • Recommend using formats designed for streaming (matroska, mpegts)
    • 



Note


Sets errno context on failure



Call ffmpeg_decoder_destroy() to cleanup




Definition at line 826 of file
    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


826                                                     {
827   // Buffer entire stdin on first call (shared between video and audio decoders)
828   if (!g_stdin_buffer) {
829     log_debug("Reading stdin into buffer...");
830     g_stdin_buffer = stdin_buffer_read_all();
831     if (!g_stdin_buffer) {
832       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_OPEN, "Failed to read stdin into buffer");
833       return NULL;
834     }
835   }
836 
837   ffmpeg_decoder_t *decoder = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(ffmpeg_decoder_t), ffmpeg_decoder_t *);
838   if (!decoder) {
839     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate decoder");
840     return NULL;
841   }
842 
843   memset(decoder, 0, sizeof(*decoder));
844   decoder->video_stream_idx = -1;
845   decoder->audio_stream_idx = -1;
846   decoder->is_stdin = true;
847   decoder->last_video_pts = -1.0;
848   decoder->last_audio_pts = -1.0;
849 
850   // Create per-decoder reader (each decoder gets independent position tracking)
851   stdin_buffer_reader_t *reader = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(stdin_buffer_reader_t), stdin_buffer_reader_t *);
852   if (!reader) {
853     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer reader");
854     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
855     return NULL;
856   }
857   reader->buffer = g_stdin_buffer;
858   reader->pos = 0;  // Each decoder starts from position 0
859 
860   // Allocate a small internal buffer for AVIO (required by avio_alloc_context)
861   decoder->avio_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(AVIO_BUFFER_SIZE, unsigned char *);
862   if (!decoder->avio_buffer) {
863     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate AVIO buffer");
864     SAFE_FREE(reader);
865     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
866     return NULL;
867   }
868 
869   decoder->avio_ctx = avio_alloc_context(decoder->avio_buffer,  // internal buffer for AVIO
870                                          AVIO_BUFFER_SIZE,       // buffer size
871                                          0,                      // write_flag
872                                          reader,                 // opaque (per-decoder reader)
873                                          memory_read_packet,
874                                          NULL,                   // write_packet
875                                          memory_seek_packet      // seek (memory is seekable)
876   );
877 
878   if (!decoder->avio_ctx) {
879     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create AVIO context");
880     SAFE_FREE(decoder->avio_buffer);
881     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
882     return NULL;
883   }
884 
885   // Mark AVIO context as seekable (memory buffer supports seeking)
886   decoder->avio_ctx->seekable = AVIO_SEEKABLE_NORMAL;
887 
888   // Allocate format context
889   decoder->format_ctx = avformat_alloc_context();
890   if (!decoder->format_ctx) {
891     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate format context");
892     av_freep(&decoder->avio_ctx->buffer);
893     avio_context_free(&decoder->avio_ctx);
894     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
895     return NULL;
896   }
897 
898   decoder->format_ctx->pb = decoder->avio_ctx;
899   decoder->format_ctx->flags |= AVFMT_FLAG_CUSTOM_IO; // Tell FFmpeg to use our custom AVIO context
900 
901   // Capture FFmpeg's probing output
902   int ret = 0;
903   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
904     // Open input from buffered stdin
905     ret = avformat_open_input(&decoder->format_ctx, NULL, NULL, NULL);
906     if (ret >= 0) {
907       // Find stream info
908       if (avformat_find_stream_info(decoder->format_ctx, NULL) < 0) {
909         ret = -1;
910       }
911     }
912   });
913 
914   if (ret < 0) {
915     if (ret == -1) {
916       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to find stream info from stdin buffer");
917     } else {
918       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_OPEN, "Failed to open stdin buffer");
919     }
920     av_freep(&decoder->avio_ctx->buffer);
921     avio_context_free(&decoder->avio_ctx);
922     if (decoder->format_ctx) {
923       avformat_free_context(decoder->format_ctx);
924     }
925     SAFE_FREE(decoder);
926     return NULL;
927   }
928 
929   // Open codecs (same as file-based decoder)
930   asciichat_error_t err = open_codec_context(decoder->format_ctx, AVMEDIA_TYPE_VIDEO, &decoder->video_stream_idx,
931                                              &decoder->video_codec_ctx);
932   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
933     log_warn("Failed to open video codec from stdin");
934   }
935 
936   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
937     err = open_codec_context(decoder->format_ctx, AVMEDIA_TYPE_AUDIO, &decoder->audio_stream_idx,
938                              &decoder->audio_codec_ctx);
939     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
940       log_warn("Failed to open audio codec from stdin");
941     }
942   } else {
943     decoder->audio_stream_idx = -1;
944     decoder->audio_codec_ctx = NULL;
945     log_debug("Audio decoding disabled by user option");
946   }
947 
948   if (decoder->video_stream_idx < 0 && decoder->audio_stream_idx < 0) {
949     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "No video or audio streams found in stdin");
950     ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
951     return NULL;
952   }
953 
954   // Allocate frame and packet
955   decoder->frame = av_frame_alloc();
956   decoder->packet = av_packet_alloc();
957   if (!decoder->frame || !decoder->packet) {
958     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
959     ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
960     return NULL;
961   }
962 
963   // Initialize swscale/swresample (same as file-based)
964   if (decoder->video_codec_ctx) {
965     // Validate codec context has valid dimensions and pixel format
966     // For stdin/HTTP streams, these might not be valid until first frame is read
967     if (decoder->video_codec_ctx->width <= 0 || decoder->video_codec_ctx->height <= 0) {
968       log_warn("Video codec has invalid dimensions (%dx%d), will initialize swscale on first frame",
969                decoder->video_codec_ctx->width, decoder->video_codec_ctx->height);
970       // Don't create swscale context yet - will create it lazily on first frame read
971     } else if (decoder->video_codec_ctx->pix_fmt == AV_PIX_FMT_NONE) {
972       log_warn("Video codec has invalid pixel format, will initialize swscale on first frame");
973       // Don't create swscale context yet - will create it lazily on first frame read
974     } else {
975       // Create swscale context with valid parameters
976       LOG_IO("swscaler", {
977         decoder->sws_ctx =
978             sws_getContext(decoder->video_codec_ctx->width, decoder->video_codec_ctx->height,
979                            decoder->video_codec_ctx->pix_fmt, decoder->video_codec_ctx->width,
980                            decoder->video_codec_ctx->height, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, SWS_BILINEAR, NULL, NULL, NULL);
981       });
982       if (!decoder->sws_ctx) {
983         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to create swscale context");
984         ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
985         return NULL;
986       }
987     }
988   }
989 
990   if (decoder->audio_codec_ctx) {
991     decoder->swr_ctx = swr_alloc();
992     if (!decoder->swr_ctx) {
993       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate swresample context");
994       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
995       return NULL;
996     }
997 
998     av_opt_set_chlayout(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_chlayout", &decoder->audio_codec_ctx->ch_layout, 0);
999     av_opt_set_int(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_sample_rate", decoder->audio_codec_ctx->sample_rate, 0);
1000     av_opt_set_sample_fmt(decoder->swr_ctx, "in_sample_fmt", decoder->audio_codec_ctx->sample_fmt, 0);
1001 
1002     AVChannelLayout out_ch_layout = AV_CHANNEL_LAYOUT_MONO;
1003     av_opt_set_chlayout(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_chlayout", &out_ch_layout, 0);
1004     av_opt_set_int(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_sample_rate", TARGET_SAMPLE_RATE, 0);
1005     av_opt_set_sample_fmt(decoder->swr_ctx, "out_sample_fmt", AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT, 0);
1006 
1007     if (swr_init(decoder->swr_ctx) < 0) {
1008       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to initialize swresample context");
1009       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1010       return NULL;
1011     }
1012 
1013     decoder->audio_buffer_size = TARGET_SAMPLE_RATE * 10;
1014     decoder->audio_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(decoder->audio_buffer_size * sizeof(float), float *);
1015     if (!decoder->audio_buffer) {
1016       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate audio buffer");
1017       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1018       return NULL;
1019     }
1020   }
1021 
1022   // Initialize video frame prefetching system for stdin (same as file-based)
1023   if (decoder->video_stream_idx >= 0) {
1024     int width = decoder->video_codec_ctx->width;
1025     int height = decoder->video_codec_ctx->height;
1026 
1027     // For stdin sources, dimensions might be invalid initially (0x0)
1028     // Use default dimensions for prefetch buffers; they'll be resized on first frame
1029     if (width <= 0 || height <= 0) {
1030       width = 1920;   // Default width for stdin
1031       height = 1080;  // Default height for stdin
1032       log_debug("stdin: Using default prefetch dimensions %dx%d (will be resized on first frame)", width, height);
1033     }
1034 
1035     // Create two prefetch image buffers for double-buffering
1036     decoder->prefetch_image_a = image_new((size_t)width, (size_t)height);
1037     decoder->prefetch_image_b = image_new((size_t)width, (size_t)height);
1038     if (!decoder->prefetch_image_a || !decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
1039       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate prefetch image buffers");
1040       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1041       return NULL;
1042     }
1043 
1044     decoder->current_prefetch_image = decoder->prefetch_image_a;
1045     decoder->prefetch_frame_ready = false;
1046 
1047     // Initialize prefetch mutex
1048     if (mutex_init(&decoder->prefetch_mutex, "ffmpeg_prefetch") != 0) {
1049       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize prefetch mutex");
1050       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1051       return NULL;
1052     }
1053 
1054     if (cond_init(&decoder->prefetch_cond, "ffmpeg_prefetch") != 0) {
1055       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize prefetch condition variable");
1056       mutex_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1057       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1058       return NULL;
1059     }
1060 
1061     if (mutex_init(&decoder->read_frame_mutex, "ffmpeg_read_frame") != 0) {
1062       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize read_frame mutex");
1063       mutex_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1064       cond_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_cond);
1065       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(decoder);
1066       return NULL;
1067     }
1068   }
1069 
1070   log_debug("FFmpeg decoder opened from stdin (video=%s, audio=%s)", decoder->video_stream_idx >= 0 ? "yes" : "no",
1071             decoder->audio_stream_idx >= 0 ? "yes" : "no");
1072 
1073   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_DECODER(decoder, "stdin", NULL);
1074 
1075   return decoder;
1076 }

void
  ffmpeg_decoder_destroy (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Destroy FFmpeg decoder and free resources.


Parameters


decoder Decoder to destroy (can be NULL)


Cleans up FFmpeg decoder and releases all resources. Safe to call
    with NULL. Automatically stops the background prefetch thread if
  running.




Cleanup process:



  
1.

  
Stop prefetch thread if running

  
2.

  
Free video frame ringbuffer

  
3.

  
Free swscale/swresample contexts

  
4.

  
Close codec contexts

  
5.

  
Close format context

  
6.

  
Free frame and packet structures

  
7.

  
Free decoder structure




Definition at line 1078 of file
    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


1078                                                        {
1079   if (!decoder) {
1080     return;
1081   }
1082 
1083   NAMED_UNREGISTER(decoder);
1084 
1085   // Stop prefetch thread (signal it to stop and wait for it to finish)
1086   if (decoder->prefetch_thread_running) {
1087     mutex_lock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1088     decoder->prefetch_should_stop = true;
1089     mutex_unlock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1090 
1091     // Wait for thread to finish
1092     asciichat_thread_join(&decoder->prefetch_thread, NULL);
1093     decoder->prefetch_thread_running = false;
1094   }
1095 
1096   // Clean up prefetch state
1097   cond_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_cond);
1098   mutex_destroy(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1099   mutex_destroy(&decoder->read_frame_mutex);
1100 
1101   // Free prefetch image buffers
1102   if (decoder->prefetch_image_a) {
1103     image_destroy(decoder->prefetch_image_a);
1104     decoder->prefetch_image_a = NULL;
1105   }
1106   if (decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
1107     image_destroy(decoder->prefetch_image_b);
1108     decoder->prefetch_image_b = NULL;
1109   }
1110 
1111   // Don't destroy current_image - it points to one of the prefetch buffers
1112   // which have already been destroyed above
1113   decoder->current_image = NULL;
1114 
1115   // Free audio buffer
1116   SAFE_FREE(decoder->audio_buffer);
1117 
1118   // Free swscale context
1119   if (decoder->sws_ctx) {
1120     sws_freeContext(decoder->sws_ctx);
1121     decoder->sws_ctx = NULL;
1122   }
1123 
1124   // Free swresample context
1125   if (decoder->swr_ctx) {
1126     swr_free(&decoder->swr_ctx);
1127   }
1128 
1129   // Free frame and packet
1130   if (decoder->frame) {
1131     av_frame_free(&decoder->frame);
1132   }
1133   if (decoder->packet) {
1134     av_packet_free(&decoder->packet);
1135   }
1136 
1137   // Free codec contexts
1138   if (decoder->video_codec_ctx) {
1139     avcodec_free_context(&decoder->video_codec_ctx);
1140   }
1141   if (decoder->audio_codec_ctx) {
1142     avcodec_free_context(&decoder->audio_codec_ctx);
1143   }
1144 
1145   // Free format context
1146   if (decoder->format_ctx) {
1147     avformat_close_input(&decoder->format_ctx);
1148   }
1149 
1150   // Free AVIO context (stdin only)
1151   if (decoder->avio_ctx) {
1152     // Free the opaque reader for stdin
1153     if (decoder->is_stdin) {
1154       SAFE_FREE(decoder->avio_ctx->opaque);
1155     }
1156     av_freep(&decoder->avio_ctx->buffer);
1157     avio_context_free(&decoder->avio_ctx);
1158   }
1159 
1160   SAFE_FREE(decoder);
1161 }

double
  ffmpeg_decoder_get_video_fps (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Get video frame rate.


Parameters


decoder Decoder (must not be NULL)


Returns


Frame rate in FPS, or -1.0 if unknown/no video

Definition at line 1297 of file
    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


1297                                                                {
1298   if (!decoder || decoder->video_stream_idx < 0) {
1299     return -1.0;
1300   }
1301 
1302   AVStream *stream = decoder->format_ctx->streams[decoder->video_stream_idx];
1303 
1304   // Try avg_frame_rate first (average frame rate from entire stream)
1305   double fps = av_q2d_safe(stream->avg_frame_rate);
1306 
1307   // Fallback to r_frame_rate if avg_frame_rate is invalid or zero
1308   // r_frame_rate is the "real" frame rate based on codec parameters
1309   // This is more reliable for YouTube videos and some video codecs
1310   if (fps <= 0.0) {
1311     fps = av_q2d_safe(stream->r_frame_rate);
1312   }
1313 
1314   return fps;
1315 }

  ffmpeg_decoder_read_audio_samples (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder, float *
  buffer, size_t num_samples)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Decode audio samples.


Parameters


decoder Decoder (must not be NULL)


buffer Output buffer for samples (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to read


Returns


Number of samples actually read (0 to num_samples)


Decodes audio samples from the media stream. Samples are resampled
    to 48kHz mono float format, compatible with ascii-chat's Opus encoding.




Audio format:


    
  
  • Format: 32-bit float
    • 
  
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
    • 
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz
    • 
  
  • Range: -1.0 to 1.0
    • 



Decoding process:



  
1.

  
Read packet from format context

  
2.

  
Send packet to audio decoder

  
3.

  
Receive decoded frame

  
4.

  
Resample to 48kHz mono using swresample

  
5.

  
Copy to output buffer




Note


Skips non-audio packets automatically



May return fewer samples than requested at EOF

    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


1321                                                                                                        {
1322   if (!decoder || decoder->audio_stream_idx < 0 || !buffer || num_samples == 0) {
1323     return 0;
1324   }
1325 
1326   size_t samples_written = 0;
1327 
1328   // First, drain any buffered samples
1329   if (decoder->audio_buffer_offset > 0) {
1330     size_t available = decoder->audio_buffer_offset;
1331     size_t to_copy = (available < num_samples) ? available : num_samples;
1332 
1333     memcpy(buffer, decoder->audio_buffer, to_copy * sizeof(float));
1334     samples_written += to_copy;
1335 
1336     // Shift buffer
1337     if (to_copy < available) {
1338       memmove(decoder->audio_buffer, decoder->audio_buffer + to_copy, (available - to_copy) * sizeof(float));
1339     }
1340     decoder->audio_buffer_offset -= to_copy;
1341 
1342     if (samples_written >= num_samples) {
1343       return samples_written;
1344     }
1345   }
1346 
1347   // Read more packets to fill the request
1348   static uint64_t packet_count = 0;
1349 
1350   // Lock read_frame_mutex to prevent concurrent av_read_frame() calls from video prefetch thread
1351   mutex_lock(&decoder->read_frame_mutex);
1352 
1353   while (samples_written < num_samples) {
1354     int ret = av_read_frame(decoder->format_ctx, decoder->packet);
1355     if (ret < 0) {
1356       if (ret == AVERROR_EOF) {
1357         decoder->eof_reached = true;
1358       }
1359       break;
1360     }
1361 
1362     // Check if this is an audio packet
1363     if (decoder->packet->stream_index != decoder->audio_stream_idx) {
1364       av_packet_unref(decoder->packet);
1365       continue;
1366     }
1367 
1368     log_info_every(50 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Audio packet #%lu: pts=%ld dts=%ld duration=%d size=%d", packet_count++,
1369                    decoder->packet->pts, decoder->packet->dts, decoder->packet->duration, decoder->packet->size);
1370 
1371     // Send packet to decoder
1372     ret = avcodec_send_packet(decoder->audio_codec_ctx, decoder->packet);
1373     av_packet_unref(decoder->packet);
1374 
1375     if (ret < 0) {
1376       log_warn("Error sending audio packet to decoder");
1377       continue;
1378     }
1379 
1380     // Receive all decoded frames from this packet
1381     // Important: a single packet can produce multiple frames. Must drain all before next packet.
1382     while (1) {
1383       ret = avcodec_receive_frame(decoder->audio_codec_ctx, decoder->frame);
1384       if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
1385         break; // No more frames from this packet, get next packet
1386       } else if (ret < 0) {
1387         log_warn("Error receiving audio frame from decoder");
1388         goto audio_read_done;
1389       }
1390 
1391       // Update position tracking
1392       decoder->last_audio_pts =
1393           get_frame_pts_seconds(decoder->frame, decoder->format_ctx->streams[decoder->audio_stream_idx]->time_base);
1394 
1395       // Resample to target format
1396       float *out_buf = buffer + samples_written;
1397       int out_samples = (int)(num_samples - samples_written);
1398 
1399       uint8_t *out_ptr = (uint8_t *)out_buf;
1400       int converted = swr_convert(decoder->swr_ctx, &out_ptr, out_samples, (const uint8_t **)decoder->frame->data,
1401                                   decoder->frame->nb_samples);
1402 
1403       if (converted > 0) {
1404         samples_written += (size_t)converted;
1405       }
1406 
1407       if (samples_written >= num_samples) {
1408         goto audio_read_done;
1409       }
1410     }
1411   }
1412 
1413 audio_read_done:
1414   // Release read_frame_mutex now that av_read_frame() is done
1415   mutex_unlock(&decoder->read_frame_mutex);
1416 
1417   // Flush resampler buffer if we haven't filled the full request
1418   // The resampler may have buffered samples that need to be output
1419   if (samples_written < num_samples) {
1420     int remaining_space = (int)(num_samples - samples_written);
1421     uint8_t *out_ptr = (uint8_t *)(buffer + samples_written);
1422     int flushed = swr_convert(decoder->swr_ctx, &out_ptr, remaining_space, NULL, 0);
1423     if (flushed > 0) {
1424       samples_written += (size_t)flushed;
1425     }
1426   }
1427 
1428   // Update sample-based position tracking
1429   decoder->audio_samples_read += samples_written;
1430 
1431   return samples_written;
1432 }

1.

  
Read packet from format context

  
2.

  
Send packet to video decoder

  
3.

  
Receive decoded frame

1169     return NULL;
1170   }
1171 
1172   // Try to get a prefetched frame from the background thread (preferred path)
1173   mutex_lock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1174   if (decoder->prefetch_frame_ready && decoder->current_prefetch_image) {
1175     // Release the previous buffer (rendering is now complete)
1176     if (decoder->current_read_buffer == decoder->prefetch_image_a) {
1177       decoder->buffer_a_in_use = false;
1178     } else if (decoder->current_read_buffer == decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
1179       decoder->buffer_b_in_use = false;
1180     }
1181 
1182     image_t *frame = decoder->current_prefetch_image;
1183     decoder->prefetch_frame_ready = false;
1184 
1185     // Mark the new buffer as in use (prevent prefetch thread from overwriting it during rendering)
1186     if (frame == decoder->prefetch_image_a) {
1187       decoder->buffer_a_in_use = true;
1188     } else if (frame == decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
1189       decoder->buffer_b_in_use = true;
1190     }
1191 
1192     decoder->current_read_buffer = frame;
1193     mutex_unlock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1194 
1195     // Use the prefetched frame
1196     decoder->current_image = frame;
1197     log_dev_every(5 * US_PER_SEC_INT, "Using prefetched frame");
1198     return frame;
1199   }
1200   mutex_unlock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1201 
1202   // No fallback synchronous decode - rely on background prefetch thread
1203   // Skipping frames when prefetch not ready allows audio timing to advance
1204   // This is critical for proper audio-video sync when prefetch is active
1205   log_dev_every(5 * US_PER_SEC_INT,
1206                 "Prefetch frame not ready, skipping to next iteration (allow prefetch to catch up)");
1207   return NULL;
1208 }

2.

3.

  
Clear decoder state

Some formats may not support seeking

  (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder, double timestamp_sec)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Seek to specific timestamp in media.


Parameters


decoder Decoder (must not be NULL)


timestamp_sec Seek target in seconds


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Seeks to the specified timestamp in the media file. Used for
    audio/video synchronization in multi-decoder scenarios.




Seek process:



  
1.

  
Flush codec buffers (avcodec_flush_buffers)

  
2.

  
Seek to timestamp (av_seek_frame with AV_TIME_BASE conversion)

  
3.

  
Clear decoder state




Note


Stdin decoders cannot seek (returns ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED)



Some formats may not support seeking




Seeks approximately - may land before/after exact timestamp




Definition at line 1473 of file
    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.


1473                                                                                                     {
1474   if (!decoder) {
1475     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1476   }
1477 
1478   if (decoder->is_stdin) {
1479     return ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED; // Cannot seek stdin
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Hold mutex during entire seek operation to prevent race with prefetch thread
1483   mutex_lock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1484 
1485   // Set flag to pause prefetch thread via condition variable
1486   decoder->seeking_in_progress = true;
1487 
1488   // Convert seconds to FFmpeg time base units (AV_TIME_BASE = 1,000,000)
1489   int64_t target_ts = (int64_t)(timestamp_sec * AV_TIME_BASE);
1490 
1491   // For HTTP streams, use simple keyframe seeking (faster than frame-accurate seeking)
1492   // HTTP seeking is expensive and can break stream state, so prefer speed over precision
1493   int seek_ret = av_seek_frame(decoder->format_ctx, -1, target_ts, AVSEEK_FLAG_BACKWARD);
1494   if (seek_ret < 0) {
1495     // Fallback: try without backward flag
1496     seek_ret = av_seek_frame(decoder->format_ctx, -1, target_ts, 0);
1497   }
1498 
1499   if (seek_ret < 0) {
1500     decoder->seeking_in_progress = false;
1501     cond_signal(&decoder->prefetch_cond);
1502     mutex_unlock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1503     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_SEEK, "Failed to seek to timestamp %.2f seconds", timestamp_sec);
1504   }
1505 
1506   // Flush codec buffers AFTER seeking
1507   if (decoder->video_codec_ctx) {
1508     avcodec_flush_buffers(decoder->video_codec_ctx);
1509   }
1510   if (decoder->audio_codec_ctx) {
1511     avcodec_flush_buffers(decoder->audio_codec_ctx);
1512   }
1513 
1514   // Reset state
1515   decoder->eof_reached = false;
1516   decoder->audio_buffer_offset = 0;
1517   // Clear any stale audio data in buffer
1518   if (decoder->audio_buffer) {
1519     memset(decoder->audio_buffer, 0, decoder->audio_buffer_size * sizeof(float));
1520   }
1521   decoder->last_video_pts = -1.0;
1522   decoder->last_audio_pts = -1.0;
1523   decoder->prefetch_frame_ready = false;
1524   // Set audio_samples_read to match the seek target so position tracking works correctly
1525   decoder->audio_samples_read = (uint64_t)(timestamp_sec * decoder->audio_sample_rate);
1526 
1527   // Reset current_read_buffer and mark both buffers as not in use
1528   // After seeking, the prefetch thread may reallocate buffers, so current_read_buffer
1529   // could point to freed memory. Clear it so the next read doesn't try to release stale pointers.
1530   decoder->current_read_buffer = NULL;
1531   decoder->buffer_a_in_use = false;
1532   decoder->buffer_b_in_use = false;
1533 
1534   // Resume prefetch thread
1535   decoder->seeking_in_progress = false;
1536   cond_signal(&decoder->prefetch_cond);
1537 
1538   // Release mutex - prefetch thread can resume
1539   mutex_unlock(&decoder->prefetch_mutex);
1540 
1541   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1542 }

void
  ffmpeg_decoder_set_exit_callback (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder,
  bool(* should_exit_callback )(void *), void * user_data)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Set exit signal callback for graceful shutdown during I/O.


Parameters


decoder Decoder (must not be NULL)


callback Function that returns true if app should exit (can be NULL)


user_data User data to pass to callback (can be NULL)


Sets a callback that allows the decoder's background prefetch
    thread to gracefully abort blocking I/O operations (like YouTube HTTP
    requests) when the application is shutting down (e.g., when user presses
    Ctrl+C).




When the callback returns true, the FFmpeg interrupt handler will
    abort any blocking av_read_frame() call, allowing the prefetch thread to
    exit cleanly rather than hanging indefinitely.




Note


Called automatically from media_source layer (no need to
  call manually)

    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.

1264   if (!decoder) {
1265     return;
1266   }
1267   decoder->should_exit_callback = should_exit_callback;
1268   decoder->exit_callback_user_data = user_data;
1269 }

    ffmpeg_decoder_t::should_exit_callback.

asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_decoder_start_prefetch
  (ffmpeg_decoder_t * decoder)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/media/ffmpeg_decoder.h>


Start background frame prefetching thread.


Parameters


decoder Decoder (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Starts a background thread that continuously reads and decodes
    video frames, storing them in an internal ringbuffer. The render loop then
    pulls pre-decoded frames from this buffer without blocking on network
  I/O.




Purpose: Solves YouTube HTTP streaming performance issues
    by decoupling the blocking av_read_frame() calls from the render loop.
    Without this, frames take 93ms to arrive instead of the expected 41.7ms at
    24 FPS.




Note


Should be called after decoder creation for FILE/HTTP
  sources



Automatically called when media source is created




Thread is stopped in ffmpeg_decoder_destroy()




Start background frame prefetching thread.




Starts a background thread that continuously reads and decodes
    video frames, storing them in double-buffer structures. The render loop
    pulls frames from these buffers. This prevents the render thread from
    blocking on YouTube HTTP requests.

    media/ffmpeg_decoder.c.

1222   if (!decoder->prefetch_image_a || !decoder->prefetch_image_b) {
1223     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1224   }
1225 
1226   // Already running
1227   if (decoder->prefetch_thread_running) {
1228     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1229   }
1230 
1231   // Reset stop flag and create thread
1232   decoder->prefetch_should_stop = false;
1233 
1234   int thread_err = asciichat_thread_create(&decoder->prefetch_thread, "ffmpeg_prefetch",
1235                                            ffmpeg_decoder_prefetch_thread_func, decoder);
1236   if (thread_err != 0) {
1237     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create video prefetch thread");
1238   }
1239 
1240   decoder->prefetch_thread_running = true;
1241   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1242 }

bool media_source_at_end (media_source_t *
  source)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Check if media source reached end of stream.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if end reached, false otherwise


Determines if the media source has reached end of stream. This
    distinguishes between read errors and legitimate EOF conditions.




Return values:


    
  
  • true: End of stream reached (no more data)
    • 
  
  • false: More data available or error occurred
    • 



Note


WEBCAM/TEST sources never reach end (always return false)



FILE/STDIN sources return true when all packets consumed




If loop is enabled, this returns false (EOF triggers rewind)

773   if (!source) {
774     return true;
775   }
776 
777   switch (source->type) {
778   case MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM:
779   case MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST:
780     return false; // Webcam never ends
781 
782   case MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE:
783   case MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN:
784     if (!source->video_decoder) {
785       return true;
786     }
787     // If loop is enabled, we never truly reach end
788     if (source->loop_enabled && source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
789       return false;
790     }
791     return ffmpeg_decoder_at_end(source->video_decoder);
792 
793   default:
794     return true;
795   }
796 }

    MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN, MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM, media_source_t::type, and
    media_source_t::video_decoder.

media_source_t * media_source_create
  (media_source_type_t type, const char * path)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Create a new media source.


Parameters


type Media source type


path File path (for FILE type), device index string (for WEBCAM), or
  NULL


Returns


Pointer to media source, or NULL on failure


Creates and initializes a media source of the specified type.




Path parameter usage:


    
  
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE: File path (e.g., "video.mp4")
    • 
  
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN: Use "-" or NULL
    • 
  
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM: Device index as string (e.g., "0") or NULL
      for default
    • 
  
  • MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST: Ignored (use NULL)
    • 



Note


For WEBCAM type, delegates to webcam_init()



For FILE/STDIN types, uses FFmpeg decoder




Call media_source_destroy() to cleanup




Definition at line 179 of file source.c.


179                                                                                 {
180   media_source_t *source = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(media_source_t), media_source_t *);
181   if (!source) {
182     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate media source");
183     return NULL;
184   }
185 
186   memset(source, 0, sizeof(*source));
187   source->type = type;
188   source->loop_enabled = false;
189   source->is_paused = false;
190 
191   // Initialize mutex for protecting shared decoder access (for YouTube URLs)
192   if (mutex_init(&source->decoder_mutex, "media_decoder") != 0) {
193     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize mutex");
194     SAFE_FREE(source);
195     return NULL;
196   }
197 
198   // Initialize mutex for protecting pause state (accessed from keyboard and video threads)
199   if (mutex_init(&source->pause_mutex, "media_pause") != 0) {
200     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize pause mutex");
201     mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
202     SAFE_FREE(source);
203     return NULL;
204   }
205 
206   // Initialize mutex for protecting decoder access during seeks (prevents race conditions)
207   if (mutex_init(&source->seek_access_mutex, "media_seek") != 0) {
208     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize seek access mutex");
209     mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
210     mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
211     SAFE_FREE(source);
212     return NULL;
213   }
214 
215   switch (type) {
216   case MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM: {
217     // Parse webcam index from path (if provided)
218     unsigned short int index = 0;
219     if (path) {
220       char *endptr;
221       errno = 0;
222       long parsed = strtol(path, &endptr, 10);
223       if (*endptr == '\0' && errno == 0 && parsed >= 0 && parsed <= USHRT_MAX) {
224         index = (unsigned short int)parsed;
225       } else if (*endptr != '\0' || errno != 0) {
226         log_error("media_source_create: Invalid webcam index: %s", path);
227       }
228     }
229 
230     source->webcam_index = index;
231     asciichat_error_t webcam_error = webcam_init_context(&source->webcam_ctx, index);
232     if (webcam_error != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
233       // Webcam init failed - log and cleanup
234       log_error("Failed to initialize webcam device %u (error code: %d)", index, webcam_error);
235       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
236       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
237       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
238       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
239       SAFE_FREE(source);
240 
241       // Explicitly re-set errno to preserve the specific error code for the caller
242       // (log_error or other calls may have cleared the thread-local errno)
243       if (webcam_error == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE) {
244         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE, "Webcam device %u is in use", index);
245       } else {
246         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Failed to initialize webcam device %u", index);
247       }
248       return NULL;
249     }
250 
251     log_debug("Media source: Webcam device %u", index);
252     break;
253   }
254 
255   case MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE: {
256     if (!path || path[0] == '\0') {
257       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "File path is required for FILE source");
258       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
259       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
260       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
261       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
262       SAFE_FREE(source);
263       return NULL;
264     }
265 
266     // Resolve URL using smart FFmpeg/yt-dlp routing
267     const char *effective_path = path;
268     char resolved_url[BUFFER_SIZE_XLARGE] = {0};
269     const char *yt_dlp_options = GET_OPTION(yt_dlp_options);
270 
271     log_debug("Resolving URL: %s", path);
272     asciichat_error_t resolve_err = media_source_resolve_url(path, yt_dlp_options, resolved_url, sizeof(resolved_url));
273     if (resolve_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
274       log_debug("Failed to resolve URL (error: %d)", resolve_err);
275       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
276       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
277       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
278       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
279       SAFE_FREE(source);
280       return NULL;
281     }
282     effective_path = resolved_url;
283     log_debug("Using resolved URL for playback");
284 
285     // Store original URL for potential re-extraction if needed
286     source->original_youtube_url = platform_strdup(path);
287     if (!source->original_youtube_url) {
288       log_warn("Failed to cache original URL");
289     }
290 
291     // Cache file path for potential reopen on loop
292     source->file_path = platform_strdup(effective_path);
293     if (!source->file_path) {
294       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to duplicate file path");
295       SAFE_FREE(source->original_youtube_url);
296       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
297       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
298       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
299       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
300       SAFE_FREE(source);
301       return NULL;
302     }
303 
304     // Always use separate decoders for video and audio
305     // This allows independent read rates and avoids lock contention
306     source->video_decoder = ffmpeg_decoder_create(effective_path);
307     if (!source->video_decoder) {
308       log_error("Failed to open media file for video: %s", effective_path);
309       SAFE_FREE(source->file_path);
310       SAFE_FREE(source->original_youtube_url);
311       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
312       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
313       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
314       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
315       SAFE_FREE(source);
316       return NULL;
317     }
318 
319     // Start prefetch thread for video frames (critical for HTTP performance)
320     // This thread continuously reads frames into a buffer so the render loop never blocks
321     asciichat_error_t prefetch_err = ffmpeg_decoder_start_prefetch(source->video_decoder);
322     if (prefetch_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
323       log_error("Failed to start video prefetch thread: %s", asciichat_error_string(prefetch_err));
324       // Don't fail on prefetch error - continue with frame skipping as fallback
325     }
326 
327     source->audio_decoder = ffmpeg_decoder_create(effective_path);
328     if (!source->audio_decoder) {
329       log_error("Failed to open media file for audio: %s", effective_path);
330       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(source->video_decoder);
331       source->video_decoder = NULL;
332       SAFE_FREE(source->file_path);
333       SAFE_FREE(source->original_youtube_url);
334       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
335       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
336       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
337       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
338       SAFE_FREE(source);
339       return NULL;
340     }
341     source->is_shared_decoder = false;
342 
343     log_debug("Media source: URL resolved to stream (separate video/audio decoders)");
344     break;
345   }
346 
347   case MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN: {
348     // Create separate decoders for video and audio from stdin
349     source->video_decoder = ffmpeg_decoder_create_stdin();
350     if (!source->video_decoder) {
351       log_error("Failed to open stdin for video input");
352       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
353       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
354       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
355       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
356       SAFE_FREE(source);
357       return NULL;
358     }
359 
360     // Start prefetch thread for stdin video frames
361     asciichat_error_t prefetch_err = ffmpeg_decoder_start_prefetch(source->video_decoder);
362     if (prefetch_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
363       log_error("Failed to start stdin video prefetch thread: %s", asciichat_error_string(prefetch_err));
364       // Don't fail on prefetch error - continue with frame skipping as fallback
365     }
366 
367     source->audio_decoder = ffmpeg_decoder_create_stdin();
368     if (!source->audio_decoder) {
369       log_error("Failed to open stdin for audio input");
370       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(source->video_decoder);
371       source->video_decoder = NULL;
372       // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
373       mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
374       mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
375       mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
376       SAFE_FREE(source);
377       return NULL;
378     }
379 
380     log_debug("Media source: stdin (separate video/audio decoders)");
381     break;
382   }
383 
384   case MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST: {
385     // Test pattern state is encapsulated in media_source_t
386     source->webcam_index = 0;
387     source->webcam_ctx = NULL; // No context needed for test pattern
388     source->test_pattern_frame = NULL;  // Allocated lazily on first read
389     source->test_frame_counter = 0;
390 
391     log_debug("Media source: Test pattern");
392     break;
393   }
394 
395   default:
396     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown media source type: %d", type);
397     // Destroy mutexes before freeing source
398     mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
399     mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
400     mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
401     SAFE_FREE(source);
402     return NULL;
403   }
404 
405   /* Register media source with named registry */
406   char media_type_str[64];
407   switch (type) {
408   case MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM:
409     snprintf(media_type_str, sizeof(media_type_str), "media_source:webcam");
410     break;
411   case MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE:
412     snprintf(media_type_str, sizeof(media_type_str), "media_source:file");
413     break;
414   case MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST:
415     snprintf(media_type_str, sizeof(media_type_str), "media_source:test");
416     break;
417   default:
418     snprintf(media_type_str, sizeof(media_type_str), "media_source:unknown");
419     break;
420   }
421   NAMED_REGISTER(source, media_type_str, "media_source_t", "0x%tx", NULL);
422 
423   /* Register media source's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
424   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&source->decoder_mutex, "decoder_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(source));
425   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&source->pause_mutex, "pause_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(source));
426   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&source->seek_access_mutex, "seek_access_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(source));
427 
428   return source;
429 }

void
  media_source_destroy (media_source_t * source)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Destroy media source and free resources.


Parameters


source Media source to destroy (can be NULL)


Cleans up media source and releases all resources. Safe to call
    with NULL.




Note


For WEBCAM type, calls webcam_destroy()



For FILE/STDIN types, closes FFmpeg decoder




Definition at line 431 of file source.c.


431                                                   {
432   if (!source) {
433     return;
434   }
435 
436   // Cleanup based on type
437   if (source->webcam_ctx) {
438     webcam_cleanup_context(source->webcam_ctx);
439     source->webcam_ctx = NULL;
440   }
441 
442   // For YouTube (shared decoder), only destroy once
443   // For local files (separate decoders), destroy both
444   if (source->video_decoder) {
445     ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(source->video_decoder);
446     source->video_decoder = NULL;
447   }
448 
449   if (source->audio_decoder) {
450     // Don't double-free if audio and video share the same decoder
451     if (!source->is_shared_decoder) {
452       ffmpeg_decoder_destroy(source->audio_decoder);
453     }
454     source->audio_decoder = NULL;
455   }
456 
457   if (source->file_path) {
458     free(source->file_path);
459     source->file_path = NULL;
460   }
461 
462   if (source->original_youtube_url) {
463     free(source->original_youtube_url);
464     source->original_youtube_url = NULL;
465   }
466 
467   // Clean up test pattern frame
468   if (source->test_pattern_frame) {
469     image_destroy(source->test_pattern_frame);
470     source->test_pattern_frame = NULL;
471   }
472 
473   // Destroy mutexes
474   mutex_destroy(&source->decoder_mutex);
475   mutex_destroy(&source->pause_mutex);
476   mutex_destroy(&source->seek_access_mutex);
477 
478   SAFE_FREE(source);
479 }

double
  media_source_get_duration (media_source_t * source)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Get media duration in seconds.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


Returns


Duration in seconds, or -1.0 if unknown/infinite


Returns the total duration of the media. Useful for progress
    display.




Return values:


    
  
  • Positive value: Duration in seconds (e.g., 120.5 for 2 minutes)
    • 
  
  • -1.0: Duration unknown (stdin, live streams) or infinite (webcam)
    • 



Note


WEBCAM/TEST sources return -1.0 (infinite)



FILE sources return actual duration from container




STDIN sources return -1.0 (cannot determine duration)

void
  media_source_pause (media_source_t * source)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Pause media playback.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


Pauses the media source. When paused, read_video and read_audio
    return no new data (NULL/silence) while maintaining the current playback
    position. Resume with media_source_resume() to continue from the
    paused position.




Behavior:


    
  
  • media_source_read_video() returns NULL when paused
    • 
  
  • media_source_read_audio() returns 0 (silence) when paused
    • 
  
  • Playback position is preserved (no seeking occurs)
    • 



Note


All source types support pause (has no effect for
  WEBCAM/TEST)



Pausing is instantaneous (next read will return no data)

  media_source_read_audio (media_source_t * source, float * buffer,
  size_t num_samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Read audio samples from media source.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


buffer Output buffer for audio samples (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to read


Returns


Number of samples actually read (may be less than
  requested)


Reads audio samples from the media source. Samples are in 32-bit
    float format, mono channel, 48kHz sample rate (compatible with Opus

  • Format: 32-bit float
    •

    • 
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz
    • 
  
  • Range: -1.0 to 1.0

  • Returns actual number of samples read (0 to num_samples)
    • 
  
  • Returns 0 on error or end of stream
    • 
  
  • Check media_source_at_end() to distinguish error from EOF

Note


WEBCAM type does NOT provide audio (returns 0)



FILE/STDIN types decode audio via FFmpeg if stream exists




Audio is resampled to 48kHz mono automatically

654     return 0;
655   }
656 
657   static uint64_t call_count = 0;
658   call_count++;
659   if (call_count <= 5 || call_count % 1000 == 0) {
660     log_info("media_source_read_audio #%lu: source_type=%d num_samples=%zu", call_count, source->type, num_samples);
661   }
662 
663   // Check pause state (thread-safe)
664   mutex_lock(&source->pause_mutex);
665   bool is_paused = source->is_paused;
666   mutex_unlock(&source->pause_mutex);
667 
668   // Return silence immediately if paused (maintaining position)
669   if (is_paused) {
670     memset(buffer, 0, num_samples * sizeof(float));
671     return num_samples;
672   }
673

675   case MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM:
676   case MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST:
677     // Webcam/test pattern don't provide audio

679

681   case MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN: {

685     }
686

692       mutex_lock(&source->decoder_mutex);
693     }
694

696     size_t samples_read = ffmpeg_decoder_read_audio_samples(source->audio_decoder, buffer, num_samples);
697     double audio_pos_after_read = ffmpeg_decoder_get_position(source->audio_decoder);
698 
699     static double last_audio_pos = 0;
700     if (audio_pos_after_read >= 0 && last_audio_pos >= 0 && audio_pos_after_read < last_audio_pos) {
701       log_warn("AUDIO POSITION WENT BACKWARD: %.2f → %.2f (LOOPING!)", last_audio_pos, audio_pos_after_read);
702     }
703     if (audio_pos_after_read >= 0) {
704       last_audio_pos = audio_pos_after_read;
705     }
706 
707     log_info_every(100 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Audio: read %zu samples, pos %.2f → %.2f", samples_read, audio_pos_before_read,
708                    audio_pos_after_read);
709 
710     // Handle EOF with loop

712       if (source->loop_enabled && source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
713         log_debug("End of file reached (audio), rewinding for loop");
714         if (media_source_rewind(source) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
715           // Try reading again after rewind
716           samples_read = ffmpeg_decoder_read_audio_samples(source->audio_decoder, buffer, num_samples);
717         }
718       }
719     }
720 
721     // Unlock shared decoder
722     if (source->is_shared_decoder) {
723       mutex_unlock(&source->decoder_mutex);
724     }
725 
726     // Release seek_access_mutex
727     mutex_unlock(&source->seek_access_mutex);
728 
729     return samples_read;
730   }
731 
732   default:
733     return 0;
734   }
735 }

  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Read next video frame from media source.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to image_t frame, or NULL on
  error/end


Reads the next video frame from the media source. Returns an
    image_t structure in RGB format, compatible with existing ascii-chat
    pipeline.




Return values:


    
  
  • Non-NULL: Valid frame pointer (do NOT free - internal buffer)
    • 
  
  • NULL: Error or end of stream (check media_source_at_end())
    • 



Frame ownership:


    
  
  • Returned frame is owned by media source
    • 
  
  • Do NOT free the returned frame
    • 
  
  • Frame is valid until next call or source destruction
    • 



Note


For WEBCAM type, calls webcam_read()



For FILE/STDIN types, decodes next video frame via FFmpeg




Frame timing is handled by caller (capture thread)

485                                                          {
486   if (!source) {
487     return NULL;
488   }
489 
490   // Check pause state (thread-safe)
491   mutex_lock(&source->pause_mutex);
492   bool is_paused = source->is_paused;
493   mutex_unlock(&source->pause_mutex);
494 
495   // Return NULL immediately if paused (maintaining position)
496   if (is_paused) {
497     return NULL;
498   }
499 
500   switch (source->type) {
501   case MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM:
502     // Read from webcam
503     if (source->webcam_ctx) {
504       return webcam_read_context(source->webcam_ctx);
505     }
506     return NULL;
507 
508   case MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST: {
509     // Generate animated test pattern in-place (animated color bars)
510     if (!source->test_pattern_frame) {
511       source->test_pattern_frame = image_new(320, 240);
512       if (!source->test_pattern_frame) {
513         return NULL;
514       }
515     }
516 
517     // Generate animated color bars that shift based on frame counter
518     unsigned int animation_phase = source->test_frame_counter / 2;  // Slow down animation
519     source->test_frame_counter++;
520 
521     for (int y = 0; y < source->test_pattern_frame->h; y++) {
522       for (int x = 0; x < source->test_pattern_frame->w; x++) {
523         rgb_pixel_t *pixel = &source->test_pattern_frame->pixels[y * source->test_pattern_frame->w + x];
524 
525         // Animated color bars that shift based on frame counter
526         int animated_x = (x + animation_phase) % source->test_pattern_frame->w;
527         int grid_x = animated_x / 40;
528 
529         // Base pattern: color bars that animate horizontally
530         switch (grid_x % 3) {
531         case 0:  // Red
532           pixel->r = 255;
533           pixel->g = 0;
534           pixel->b = 0;
535           break;
536         case 1:  // Green
537           pixel->r = 0;
538           pixel->g = 255;
539           pixel->b = 0;
540           break;
541         case 2:  // Blue
542         default:
543           pixel->r = 0;
544           pixel->g = 0;
545           pixel->b = 255;
546           break;
547         }
548 
549         // Add animated grid lines
550         if (animated_x % 40 == 0 || y % 30 == 0) {
551           pixel->r = 0;
552           pixel->g = 0;
553           pixel->b = 0;
554         }
555       }
556     }
557 
558     return source->test_pattern_frame;
559   }
560 
561   case MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE:
562   case MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN: {
563     if (!source->video_decoder) {
564       return NULL;
565     }
566 
567     // Lock shared decoder if YouTube URL (protect against concurrent audio thread access)
568     if (source->is_shared_decoder) {
569       mutex_lock(&source->decoder_mutex);
570     }
571 
572     uint64_t frame_read_start_ns = time_get_ns();
573     image_t *frame = ffmpeg_decoder_read_video_frame(source->video_decoder);
574     uint64_t frame_read_ns = time_elapsed_ns(frame_read_start_ns, time_get_ns());
575 
576     // Track frame reading statistics for FPS diagnosis
577     static uint64_t total_attempts = 0;
578     static uint64_t successful_frames = 0;
579     static uint64_t null_frame_count = 0;
580     static uint64_t total_read_time_ns = 0;
581     static uint64_t max_read_time_ns = 0;
582 
583     total_attempts++;
584     if (frame) {
585       successful_frames++;
586       total_read_time_ns += frame_read_ns;
587       if (frame_read_ns > max_read_time_ns) {
588         max_read_time_ns = frame_read_ns;
589       }
590 
591       // Log statistics every 30 successful frames
592       if (successful_frames % 30 == 0) {
593         double avg_read_ms = (double)total_read_time_ns / (double)successful_frames / NS_PER_MS;
594         double max_read_ms = (double)max_read_time_ns / NS_PER_MS;
595         double null_rate = (double)null_frame_count * 100.0 / (double)total_attempts;
596         log_info_every(3 * US_PER_SEC_INT,
597                        "FRAME_STATS[%lu]: avg_read=%.2f ms, max_read=%.2f ms, null_rate=%.1f%% "
598                        "(%lu null/%lu attempts)",
599                        successful_frames, avg_read_ms, max_read_ms, null_rate, null_frame_count, total_attempts);
600       }
601     } else {
602       null_frame_count++;
603     }
604 
605     // Handle EOF with loop
606     if (!frame && ffmpeg_decoder_at_end(source->video_decoder)) {
607       if (source->loop_enabled && source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
608         log_debug("End of file reached, rewinding for loop");
609         if (media_source_rewind(source) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
610           // Try reading again after rewind
611           frame = ffmpeg_decoder_read_video_frame(source->video_decoder);
612         }
613       }
614     }
615 
616     // Unlock shared decoder
617     if (source->is_shared_decoder) {
618       mutex_unlock(&source->decoder_mutex);
619     }
620 
621     return frame;
622   }
623 
624   default:
625     return NULL;
626   }
627 }

824       return video_result;

828     // For local files (separate decoders), rewind audio too

831       result = ffmpeg_decoder_rewind(source->audio_decoder);
832     }
833 
834     // Unlock shared decoder
835     if (source->is_shared_decoder) {
836       mutex_unlock(&source->decoder_mutex);
837     }
838 
839     return result;
840 
841   case MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN:
842     return ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED; // Cannot seek stdin
843 
844   default:
845     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
846   }
847 }

asciichat_error_t media_source_seek (media_source_t
  * source, double timestamp_sec)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Seek media source to timestamp.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


timestamp_sec Timestamp in seconds


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Seeks both video and audio decoders to the specified timestamp.
    Handles shared decoder locking for YouTube URLs.




Behavior:


    
  
  • FILE sources: Seek to timestamp (if seekable)
    • 
  
  • STDIN sources: ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED (cannot seek stdin)
    • 
  
  • WEBCAM/TEST sources: No-op (always returns OK)
    • 



Thread safety:


    
  
  • For YouTube URLs with shared decoder, automatically locks during seek
    • 
  
  • Safe to call from any thread
    • 



Note


FILE sources only (returns OK for others)



STDIN sources: ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED (cannot seek stdin)




Negative timestamps are rejected




Seeks are approximate (may land before requested timestamp)




Definition at line 849 of file source.c.


849                                                                                   {
850   if (!source) {
851     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Media source is NULL");
852     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
853   }
854 
855   if (timestamp_sec < 0.0) {
856     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Timestamp must be >= 0.0");
857     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
858   }
859 
860   // WEBCAM and TEST sources: no-op (always return OK)
861   if (source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM || source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST) {
862     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
863   }
864 
865   // STDIN: not supported
866   if (source->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN) {
867     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED, "Cannot seek stdin");
868     return ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED;
869   }
870 
871   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
872 
873   // Clear audio playback buffer BEFORE seeking to prevent old audio from being queued
874   // This ensures fresh audio starts playing immediately after seek completes
875   if (source->audio_ctx) {
876     audio_context_t *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t *)source->audio_ctx;
877     if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
878       audio_ring_buffer_clear(audio_ctx->playback_buffer);
879     }
880   }
881 
882   // The seeking_in_progress flag in decoders blocks prefetch thread
883   // seeking_in_progress coordinates with audio callback via condition variable
884 
885   uint64_t seek_start_ns = time_get_ns();
886   if (source->video_decoder) {
887     double video_pos_before = ffmpeg_decoder_get_position(source->video_decoder);
888     asciichat_error_t video_err = ffmpeg_decoder_seek_to_timestamp(source->video_decoder, timestamp_sec);
889     double video_pos_after = ffmpeg_decoder_get_position(source->video_decoder);
890     uint64_t video_seek_ns = time_elapsed_ns(seek_start_ns, time_get_ns());
891     char video_seek_str[32];
892     time_pretty(video_seek_ns, -1, video_seek_str, sizeof(video_seek_str));
893     if (video_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
894       log_warn("Video seek to %.2f failed: error code %d (took %s)", timestamp_sec, video_err, video_seek_str);
895       result = video_err;
896     } else {
897       log_info("Video SEEK: %.2f → %.2f sec (target %.2f, took %s)", video_pos_before, video_pos_after, timestamp_sec,
898                video_seek_str);
899     }
900   }
901 
902   // Seek audio decoder (if separate from video)
903   if (source->audio_decoder && !source->is_shared_decoder) {
904     log_info("=== Starting audio seek to %.2f sec ===", timestamp_sec);
905     uint64_t audio_seek_start_ns = time_get_ns();
906     double audio_pos_before = ffmpeg_decoder_get_position(source->audio_decoder);
907     log_info("Audio position before seek: %.2f", audio_pos_before);
908     asciichat_error_t audio_err = ffmpeg_decoder_seek_to_timestamp(source->audio_decoder, timestamp_sec);
909     double audio_pos_after = ffmpeg_decoder_get_position(source->audio_decoder);
910     uint64_t audio_seek_ns = time_elapsed_ns(audio_seek_start_ns, time_get_ns());
911     log_info("Audio position after seek: %.2f", audio_pos_after);
912     char audio_seek_str[32];
913     time_pretty(audio_seek_ns, -1, audio_seek_str, sizeof(audio_seek_str));
914     if (audio_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
915       log_warn("Audio seek to %.2f failed: error code %d (took %s)", timestamp_sec, audio_err, audio_seek_str);
916       result = audio_err;
917     } else {
918       log_info("Audio SEEK COMPLETE: %.2f → %.2f sec (target %.2f, took %s)", audio_pos_before, audio_pos_after,
919                timestamp_sec, audio_seek_str);
920     }
921   }
922 
923   // Prefetch thread automatically resumes when seeking_in_progress is cleared and signaled
924 
925   return result;
926 }

void
  media_source_set_exit_callback (media_source_t * source, bool(*
  should_exit_callback )(void *), void * user_data)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Set exit signal callback for graceful shutdown during I/O.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


callback Function that returns true if app should exit (can be NULL)


user_data User data to pass to callback (can be NULL)


Sets a callback on the underlying decoders that allows graceful
    shutdown when the application is shutting down (e.g., Ctrl+C). This is
    particularly important for file/HTTP sources where av_read_frame() might
    block indefinitely.




Purpose: When the user presses Ctrl+C, the signal handler
    sets the app's exit flag. This callback is polled by FFmpeg's interrupt
    handler to abort blocking I/O operations, allowing the prefetch thread to
    exit cleanly instead of hanging indefinitely.




Note


Called automatically from session_capture_create (no need
  to call manually)

1052     ffmpeg_decoder_set_exit_callback(source->video_decoder, should_exit_callback, user_data);
1053   }
1054 
1055   // Set exit callback on audio decoder if present (only if different from video decoder)
1056   if (source->audio_decoder && source->audio_decoder != source->video_decoder) {
1057     ffmpeg_decoder_set_exit_callback(source->audio_decoder, should_exit_callback, user_data);
1058   }
1059 }

void
  media_source_set_loop (media_source_t * source, bool

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Enable or disable looping.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


loop true to enable looping, false to disable


Enables loop mode for media files. When looping is enabled, the
    media source will automatically seek to the beginning when EOF is
  reached.




Behavior:


    
  
  • FILE sources: Seek to beginning on EOF (if seekable)
    • 
  
  • STDIN sources: Looping NOT supported (cannot seek stdin)
    • 
  
  • WEBCAM/TEST sources: Ignored (infinite by nature)
    • 



Note


For stdin, loop flag is silently ignored



Looping works for both video and audio streams

  media_source_toggle_pause (media_source_t * source)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/media/source.h>


Toggle pause state of media source.


Parameters


source Media source (must not be NULL)


Toggles between paused and playing states. If paused, resumes
    playback. If playing, pauses playback.
